name: Building Renovation for Energy Efficiency
description: This call supports projects aimed at accelerating the energy renovation of public and residential buildings across the EU. It funds initiatives that develop integrated renovation strategies, implement deep renovation pilots in municipal buildings, deploy smart energy management systems, and promote nearly zero-energy building (nZEB) standards. Projects should demonstrate replicable approaches to reducing energy consumption in the built environment by at least 60%, with a focus on public buildings such as schools, hospitals, and administrative offices. Technical assistance for local authorities in developing long-term renovation strategies is also eligible.

name: Solar Energy for Public Infrastructure
description: This call supports the deployment of solar photovoltaic systems on public buildings, parking structures, and municipal land. Projects should demonstrate innovative financing mechanisms such as energy communities, power purchase agreements, or citizen cooperatives for solar deployment. The call also covers the integration of solar energy with battery storage systems and smart grid technologies to optimize self-consumption in public facilities. Priority is given to projects in regions with high solar irradiance but low adoption rates, particularly in Southern Europe.

name: Smart Infrastructure for Resilient Cities
description: This topic supports research and innovation projects that develop and test smart infrastructure solutions for medium-sized European cities. Projects should integrate IoT sensor networks, digital twins, and AI-based predictive maintenance systems for critical urban infrastructure including water networks, bridges, roads, and public lighting. The solutions must be demonstrated in at least three pilot cities and include citizen engagement components. Particular attention should be paid to cybersecurity of connected infrastructure and interoperability between municipal data systems.

name: Digital Skills and Inclusive Education Partnerships
description: This call supports cooperation partnerships focused on developing digital competences in primary and secondary education. Projects should create innovative curricula integrating computational thinking, media literacy, and responsible AI use in schools. Priority is given to partnerships that address the digital divide in underserved communities, including rural areas and disadvantaged neighbourhoods. Projects must involve schools from at least three EU member states and produce open educational resources (OER) that can be freely adopted by other institutions.

name: Sustainable Urban Development in Italian Municipalities
description: Questo bando finanzia interventi integrati di sviluppo urbano sostenibile nei comuni italiani con popolazione compresa tra 15.000 e 100.000 abitanti. Sono ammessi progetti che combinano riqualificazione degli spazi pubblici, mobilità sostenibile, efficientamento energetico degli edifici pubblici e inclusione sociale. I progetti devono prevedere un approccio partecipativo con il coinvolgimento della cittadinanza nella fase di progettazione. Particolare attenzione è rivolta ai comuni del Mezzogiorno e alle aree interne. Il contributo massimo per progetto è di 3 milioni di euro.

name: Local Digital Skills Hubs
description: This call finances the creation of local digital skills hubs in municipalities across Europe. These hubs serve as physical spaces where citizens, particularly the elderly, unemployed, and digitally excluded populations, can access training on essential digital skills including e-government services, online safety, and basic programming. Projects should establish partnerships between local authorities, libraries, community centres, and private sector training providers. Each hub must serve a minimum of 500 citizens annually and offer training in the local language.

name: Sustainable Urban Mobility Plans Implementation
description: This call supports municipalities in implementing their Sustainable Urban Mobility Plans (SUMPs). Eligible actions include the deployment of electric bus fleets, creation of cycling infrastructure networks, implementation of low-emission zones, installation of electric vehicle charging stations, and development of multimodal transport hubs. Projects must demonstrate a measurable reduction in transport-related CO2 emissions of at least 20% within the project area. Co-financing rate is up to 85% for cohesion regions.

name: Urban Biodiversity and Green Infrastructure
description: This call supports projects that protect and restore urban and peri-urban biodiversity through nature-based solutions. Eligible actions include the creation of urban forests, green corridors connecting fragmented habitats, rewilding of brownfield sites, restoration of urban waterways, and implementation of pollinator-friendly municipal landscaping policies. Projects should demonstrate measurable increases in local biodiversity indices and engage citizens through community science programmes. Replication plans in at least two additional municipalities are required.

name: Youth Employment and Skills Development Pathways
description: This call finances programmes that improve employment prospects for young people aged 15-29 who are not in education, employment, or training (NEETs). Projects should combine vocational training, digital skills development, mentorship, and work placements in local businesses. Special emphasis is placed on green skills (energy auditing, sustainable construction, circular economy) and digital skills (data analysis, web development, cybersecurity). Projects must achieve a minimum employment rate of 40% among participants within six months of programme completion.

name: Sustainable Tourism in Mediterranean Coastal Communities
description: This call supports transnational cooperation projects that develop sustainable tourism models in Mediterranean coastal municipalities. Projects should address overtourism, seasonality, and environmental degradation while promoting cultural heritage, local gastronomy, and eco-tourism. Eligible actions include digital tourism platforms, visitor management systems, heritage trail development, and community-based tourism initiatives. Projects must involve partners from at least three Mediterranean countries and demonstrate transferable approaches to sustainable destination management.

name: Community Health Innovation Ecosystems
description: This topic supports the creation of local health innovation ecosystems connecting municipal health services with digital health startups and research institutions. Projects should develop and pilot telemedicine platforms for rural and underserved areas, AI-assisted early screening tools for common conditions, and community health data platforms that respect GDPR requirements. Solutions must be co-designed with healthcare professionals and patients. At least 30% of the budget should target digital health literacy among the elderly population.

name: Circular Economy and Zero-Waste Municipalities
description: This call supports municipalities in transitioning towards zero-waste models through circular economy approaches. Eligible actions include the deployment of smart waste collection systems using IoT sensors, development of local material recovery and repair facilities, implementation of pay-as-you-throw schemes, food waste prevention programmes connecting restaurants and food banks, and municipal composting facilities. Projects should demonstrate a reduction in residual waste generation of at least 30% and an increase in recycling rates to above 65%.

name: Rigenerazione dei Borghi e delle Aree Interne
description: Il bando finanzia interventi di rigenerazione culturale, sociale ed economica nei borghi italiani e nelle aree interne a rischio spopolamento. Sono ammessi progetti che includono il recupero del patrimonio edilizio storico, la creazione di spazi di co-working e residenze artistiche, il potenziamento della connettività digitale (banda ultra-larga), la valorizzazione delle produzioni locali e dell'artigianato, e iniziative per attrarre nuovi residenti e lavoratori da remoto. I comuni beneficiari devono avere una popolazione inferiore a 5.000 abitanti e dimostrare un tasso di spopolamento superiore al 10% nell'ultimo decennio.

name: Broadband Connectivity for Underserved Areas
description: This call supports the deployment of very high capacity broadband networks (at least 1 Gbps) in rural, remote, and underserved areas across the EU. Projects should cover last-mile connectivity, including fibre-to-the-home (FTTH) deployments, fixed wireless access solutions, and satellite backhaul integration. Municipal digital services that leverage the new connectivity — such as e-government portals, smart lighting control systems, and environmental monitoring networks — are eligible as complementary actions. Priority is given to areas where no commercial operator plans to invest within the next three years.

name: Combating Energy Poverty in European Municipalities
description: This call addresses energy poverty at the local level by supporting municipalities in identifying energy-poor households and implementing targeted support measures. Eligible actions include energy audits for low-income housing, installation of energy-efficient appliances and insulation, establishment of one-stop shops for energy advice, training of social workers as energy advisors, and development of local energy poverty action plans. Projects should integrate social and energy policies and demonstrate measurable reductions in energy expenditure for vulnerable households (at least 20% savings).

name: Sustainable Local Food Systems
description: This call supports research and innovation in developing sustainable local food systems that reduce the environmental footprint of food production and distribution. Projects should demonstrate farm-to-fork approaches at the municipal level including urban farming initiatives, short supply chain platforms connecting local producers with public canteens (schools, hospitals), food waste reduction technologies, and nutritional education programmes. Projects must quantify environmental impact reduction (carbon footprint, water usage, food miles) and demonstrate economic viability for small-scale producers.

name: Civic Engagement and Participatory Democracy
description: This call supports projects that strengthen participatory democracy and civic engagement at the local level. Eligible actions include the development of digital platforms for citizen participation in municipal decision-making (participatory budgeting, civic consultations), programmes that promote democratic values and media literacy, youth civic engagement initiatives, and cross-border town twinning activities focused on shared democratic challenges. Projects must demonstrate increased citizen participation rates and improved transparency in local governance.

name: Integrated Water Management for Climate Resilience
description: This call supports integrated water management projects in municipalities facing water stress and flood risks exacerbated by climate change. Eligible actions include deployment of nature-based solutions for stormwater management (rain gardens, permeable pavements, urban wetlands), smart water network monitoring systems using IoT and AI for leak detection, water reuse systems for irrigation of public green spaces, and citizen awareness campaigns on water conservation. Projects in the Mediterranean basin receive priority scoring.

name: Gender Equality in Local Labour Markets
description: This call supports municipal-level projects that promote gender equality in local labour markets and public services. Eligible actions include establishment of municipal childcare facilities to support women's employment, women's entrepreneurship incubators, gender-responsive budgeting training for local administrators, programmes combating gender-based violence through local support networks, and mentorship programmes for women in STEM fields. Projects must include gender-disaggregated data collection and impact assessment methodologies.

name: Just Transition Support for Italian Industrial Regions
description: Questo bando supporta la transizione giusta nelle regioni italiane con elevata dipendenza da combustibili fossili o industrie ad alta intensità di carbonio. Sono finanziabili progetti di riqualificazione professionale dei lavoratori dei settori in transizione, creazione di nuove imprese nella green economy, bonifica di siti industriali dismessi, sviluppo di filiere per l'idrogeno verde e le energie rinnovabili, e programmi di accompagnamento sociale per le comunità colpite dalla transizione energetica. Le regioni eleggibili includono Taranto, Sulcis, e le aree del Centro Italia con centrali a carbone in dismissione.

name: Cultural Innovation in Small European Towns
description: This call supports cultural and creative projects in small towns and rural areas with populations under 20,000. Eligible actions include the creation of cultural hubs in repurposed public buildings, artist residency programmes that engage with local communities, digital archives of local intangible cultural heritage (oral traditions, crafts, folk music), immersive cultural experiences using AR/VR technologies, and cultural entrepreneurship programmes for young creatives. Projects must demonstrate how culture can serve as a catalyst for social cohesion and local economic development.

name: Climate Adaptation Strategies for Southern European Cities
description: This topic supports the development and testing of integrated climate adaptation strategies for cities in Southern Europe facing extreme heat, drought, and flood risks. Projects should develop urban heat island mitigation solutions (cool roofs, urban canopy strategies, misting systems in public spaces), drought-resistant urban landscaping approaches, early warning systems for extreme weather events, and heat action plans for vulnerable populations. Solutions must be co-created with local stakeholders and demonstrate cost-effectiveness compared to traditional grey infrastructure approaches.

name: Digitalizzazione delle PMI nei Comuni Italiani
description: Il bando sostiene la creazione e il rafforzamento di hub di innovazione digitale a livello comunale per supportare la trasformazione digitale delle piccole e medie imprese locali. Le azioni ammissibili includono: servizi di consulenza digitale per le PMI (e-commerce, cloud computing, analisi dei dati), formazione digitale per imprenditori e dipendenti, laboratori di prototipazione rapida e fabbricazione digitale (fab lab), programmi di mentoring tra startup tecnologiche e imprese tradizionali, e voucher digitali per l'acquisto di tecnologie innovative. Priorità ai comuni con un tessuto produttivo prevalentemente manifatturiero o artigianale.

name: Energy Communities for Municipal Buildings
description: This call supports the establishment of renewable energy communities centred around municipal buildings and public infrastructure. Projects should demonstrate how local authorities can serve as anchors for energy communities, sharing solar energy generated on public buildings with surrounding residential areas. Eligible actions include legal and technical support for community establishment, installation of shared renewable energy systems, deployment of community energy management platforms, and economic modelling of energy sharing arrangements. Projects must engage at least 100 community members and demonstrate energy cost reductions of at least 15%.

name: Local Integration Strategies for Migrants and Refugees
description: This call supports municipalities in developing and implementing comprehensive local integration strategies for migrants and refugees. Eligible actions include language training programmes combined with vocational skills development, cultural mediation services in public offices and healthcare facilities, social housing integration programmes, anti-discrimination awareness campaigns, and peer mentoring networks connecting established residents with newcomers. Projects must demonstrate measurable improvements in integration outcomes (language proficiency, employment, social participation) and include co-design with migrant communities.

name: AI Testing and Experimentation for Public Services
description: This call supports the deployment of artificial intelligence solutions in public services at the municipal level. Projects should establish AI testing and experimentation facilities where municipalities can pilot AI applications for citizen services (chatbots, automated document processing), urban planning (traffic optimization, land use prediction), environmental monitoring (air quality, noise pollution mapping), and predictive maintenance of public assets. All deployed solutions must comply with the EU AI Act and include explainability and transparency mechanisms. Human oversight and bias auditing procedures are mandatory.

name: Edilizia Residenziale Pubblica Sostenibile
description: Il bando finanzia interventi di ristrutturazione e nuova costruzione di edilizia residenziale pubblica con criteri di sostenibilità ambientale ed energetica. Sono ammessi progetti che prevedono la riqualificazione energetica profonda di complessi di edilizia popolare esistenti, la costruzione di nuovi alloggi sociali a energia quasi zero (nZEB), l'installazione di impianti fotovoltaici e sistemi di accumulo condivisi, la creazione di spazi comuni e servizi di prossimità per i residenti, e interventi di domotica assistiva per anziani e persone con disabilità. I comuni devono dimostrare un fabbisogno abitativo documentato e un piano di assegnazione trasparente.

name: Promoting Organic and Sustainable Agriculture at Local Level
description: This call supports information and promotion campaigns for organic and sustainable agricultural products at the local and regional level. Projects should establish direct links between organic producers and municipal procurement (school canteens, public hospitals), create farmers' markets and community-supported agriculture schemes, develop educational programmes on sustainable food choices for school children, and implement local food labelling and traceability systems. Priority is given to projects in regions where organic agriculture represents less than 10% of utilized agricultural area.

name: Sustainable and Digital Tourism Ecosystems
description: This call supports the development of sustainable and digital tourism ecosystems in European destinations facing challenges of seasonality, over-tourism, or under-development. Projects should implement smart destination management platforms using real-time visitor data, develop off-season tourism products (cultural routes, gastro-tourism, wellness tourism), create digital storytelling experiences for cultural heritage sites, train local tourism SMEs in digital marketing and sustainability practices, and establish visitor management systems to reduce environmental impact in sensitive areas.
